name: My business in San Diego is home-based with no storefront—can Alexa still list me, and how should I set that up?
description: Yes, Alexa can list a home-based San Diego business if your profiles are set up as a service-area business on Google and other directories, with consistent name

mainEntity.name: My business in San Diego is home-based with no storefront—can Alexa still list me, and how should I set that up?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you run a mobile or home-based service in San Diego with no public storefront, you can still show up on Alexa by setting a service area and hiding your exact address where allowed.

Alexa mainly cares that your data sources describe a real, verifiable business in that geographic area.

Key factors:
- Use service-area settings in Google Business Profile and compatible directories
- Keep NAP consistent even if your address isn’t publicly displayed everywhere
- Clearly define your services and coverage zones (e.g., “San Diego County”) in descriptions
- Maintain reviews tied to your business name and phone number

Configure your profiles as a service-area business, then test Alexa with location-specific voice queries like “near me in San Diego” from different neighborhoods.
Being transparent about where you serve and how to contact you helps Alexa route relevant queries to your listing.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, Alexa can list a home-based San Diego business if your profiles are set up as a service-area business on Google and other directories, with consistent name, phone, and clearly defined service areas. You don’t need a walk-in storefront; you need verifiable, accurate local data tied to your business.
